LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT COLETTE O'BRIEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT COLETTE O'BRIEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON HER CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. [REFER TO WORD MINUTES FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.] ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph B. Adams, Esq. of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum, T:415.403.4404, for defendants and moving party;
Craig C. Daniel, Esq., of Gluck Daniel LLP, T:415.510.2509, for plaintiff & opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT COLETTE O'BRIEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT as follows:

Defendant Colette O'Brien's motion for summary judgment on plaintiff's first amended complaint is granted. Defendant's motion for summary judgment on her cross-complaint is denied.

[Refer to word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.]

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Craig Daniel, Esq. 415-510-2509 of Gluck Daniel for Plaintiff.
Joseph Adams, Esq. 415-397-9006 of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF WAYNE ESPINOZA, ALISON ESPINOZA'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Wayne Espinoza and Alison Espinoza's motion for leave to file amended complaint is granted on the condition that Defendant Colette O'Brien need not re-notice her pending motion for summary judgment, currently set for hearing on August 21, 2019, to meet the newly-asserted causes of action in the amended complaint. If Defendant wishes to seek summary judgment or summary adjudication as to the amended complaint, she may file supplemental motion papers, which shall be filed and served no later than August 2, 2019. Plaintiffs' opposition to the motion and Defendant's reply shall remain due on the statutory schedule.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
